steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student must refute the claim that malapportionment favors small urban districts. The notes show the opposite: rural districts get disproportionate representation compared to urban ones.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — explains rural overrepresentation via the formula (1.8 points per land unit vs. 1 point per resident): This uses the formula's mechanics to show rural districts are overrepresented, directly contradicting the false claim. ✓
  • Choice B — reverses which districts are overrepresented: This restates the claim being refuted rather than contradicting it. ✗
  • Choice C — defines malapportionment without addressing the claim: A definition alone does not refute the specific claim about which districts benefit. ✗
  • Choice D — describes urban overrepresentation: This contradicts what the notes say and does not refute the claim correctly. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
